title: Capacity: paying for power plants to sit idle 95% of the time
title_translation: 容量：为95%时间闲置的发电厂买单

paragraphs.source: At a high level, a monthly household electric bill is driven by the following charges:
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: At a high level, a monthly household electric bill is driven by the following charges:
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: At a high level, a monthly household electric bill is driven by the following charges:

pro.text: 总体而言，家庭月度电费账单主要由以下几项费用构成：

gemini_refined.text: 总体而言，家庭月度电费账单主要由以下几项费用构成：

manual.text: 总体而言，家庭月度电费账单主要由以下几项费用构成：

paragraphs.confirmed: 总体而言，家庭月度电费账单主要由以下几项费用构成：

applied_terms: Terminology is critical: 'Capacity market' (容量市场), 'energy-only market' (单一电量市场/纯电能市场), 'retail adders' (零售附加费), 'ancillary services' (辅助服务).
Ensure the explanation of 'scarcity pricing' (稀缺定价) clearly conveys its role as an incentive mechanism.

understanding.role_in_article: Educates the reader on grid economics, defining the structural difference between PJM's capacity market and ERCOT's energy-only market.
understanding.relation_to_previous: Shifts the focus from the symptoms (high bills, AI blame) to the underlying structural mechanics of how electricity bills are calculated.
understanding.relation_to_next: Provides the necessary technical definitions to understand the magnitude of PJM's $16 billion failure in the subsequent section.
key_points: Capacity markets (like PJM) charge ratepayers to pay power plants to sit idle for extreme peak scenarios.
ERCOT uses an 'energy-only' model relying on real-time scarcity pricing instead of centralized annual capacity auctions.
translation_notes: Terminology is critical: 'Capacity market' (容量市场), 'energy-only market' (单一电量市场/纯电能市场), 'retail adders' (零售附加费), 'ancillary services' (辅助服务).
Ensure the explanation of 'scarcity pricing' (稀缺定价) clearly conveys its role as an incentive mechanism.

issues.description: 提示词的本章翻译注意点中明确要求将 'retail adders' 翻译为“零售附加费”，但译文中使用了“零售加价”。
原文中的 'against' 表示“相对于/以...为基数”，意在强调 2 GW 的波动幅度相对于 6-8 GW 的总峰值来说占比极大。译文将其拆分为并列句“而其日常峰值负荷通常为 6 至 8 吉瓦”，略微削弱了这种对比的张力。

paragraphs.source: Energy: In deregulated territories (including PJM’s 13 states, and ERCOT/Texas), this is a function of wholesale prices, i.e. real-time supply and demand for electricity.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: * **Energy**: In deregulated territories (including PJM’s 13 states, and ERCOT/Texas), this is a function of wholesale prices, i.e. real-time supply and demand for electricity.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Energy: In deregulated territories (including PJM’s 13 states, and ERCOT/Texas), this is a function of wholesale prices, i.e. real-time supply and demand for electricity.

pro.text: 电量：在放开管制的地区（包括PJM覆盖的13个州以及ERCOT/德克萨斯州），这项费用取决于批发价格，即电力的实时供需情况。

原文中的 'against' 表示“相对于/以...为基数”，意在强调 2 GW 的波动幅度相对于 6-8 GW 的总峰值来说占比极大。译文将其拆分为并列句“而其日常峰值负荷通常为 6 至 8 吉瓦”，略微削弱了这种对比的张力。

paragraphs.source: Capacity: This charge doesn’t exist in ERCOT but matters in PJM. It is a function of supply & demand for capacity, i.e. power that will only turn on a few hours per year, at necessary peak events. In PJM, this charge is defined annually via a large auction.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: * **Capacity**: This charge doesn’t exist in ERCOT but matters in PJM. It is a function of supply & demand for **capacity**, i.e. power that will only turn on a few hours per year, at necessary peak events. In PJM, this charge is defined annually via a large auction.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Capacity: This charge doesn’t exist in ERCOT but matters in PJM. It is a function of supply & demand for capacity, i.e. power that will only turn on a few hours per year, at necessary peak events. In PJM, this charge is defined annually via a large auction.

pro.text: 容量：这项费用在ERCOT中并不存在，但在PJM中却至关重要。它取决于容量的供需情况，即那些每年仅在必要的用电高峰期才会启动数小时的电力资源。在PJM，这项费用每年通过一场大型拍卖来确定。
pro.tokenized_text: [[[STRONG_1|容量]]]：这项费用在ERCOT中并不存在，但在PJM中却至关重要。它取决于[[[STRONG_2|容量]]]的供需情况，即那些每年仅在必要的用电高峰期才会启动数小时的电力资源。在PJM，这项费用每年通过一场大型拍卖来确定。

paragraphs.source: This remains a highly regulated area. T&D providers typically earn a pre-determined, regulated Return on Equity. As such, utilization rate of transmission & distribution assets can impact consumer power prices. We will not dive into this in this report – we’ll leave that for a future deep dive.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: > This remains a highly regulated area. T&D providers typically earn a pre-determined, regulated Return on Equity. As such, utilization rate of transmission & distribution assets can impact consumer power prices. We will not dive into this in this report – we’ll leave that for a future deep dive.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: This remains a highly regulated area. T&D providers typically earn a pre-determined, regulated Return on Equity. As such, utilization rate of transmission & distribution assets can impact consumer power prices. We will not dive into this in this report – we’ll leave that for a future deep dive.

pro.text: 这一领域依然受到高度监管。输配电企业通常赚取预先设定的、受监管的股本回报率。因此，输配电资产的利用率会影响终端消费者的电价。我们在本报告中不会对此展开探讨，而是将其留待未来的深度报告再作剖析。

Ensure the explanation of 'scarcity pricing' (稀缺定价) clearly conveys its role as an incentive mechanism.

issues.description: 提示词的本章翻译注意点中明确要求将 'retail adders' 翻译为“零售附加费”，但译文中使用了“零售加价”。
原文中的 'against' 表示“相对于/以...为基数”，意在强调 2 GW 的波动幅度相对于 6-8 GW 的总峰值来说占比极大。译文将其拆分为并列句“而其日常峰值负荷通常为 6 至 8 吉瓦”，略微削弱了这种对比的张力。

paragraphs.source: The purpose of capacity markets is to ensure households and businesses always have the electricity they need, including on peak summer or winter days. For context, on a single day, the electric load of New York City alone can vary by 2 gigawatts (GW), against a daily peak of 6-8 GW. During a heat wave, this same city can draw 10 GW of electricity as everyone runs their air conditioning at once.

inline_elements.text: New York City alone can vary by 2 gigawatts (GW

paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: The purpose of capacity markets is to ensure households and businesses always have the electricity they need, including on peak summer or winter days. For context, on a single day, the electric load of **New York City alone can vary by 2 gigawatts (GW**), against a daily peak of **6-8 GW.** During a heat wave, this same city can draw **10 GW** of electricity as everyone runs their air conditioning at once.

pro.text: 容量市场的设立初衷，是确保家庭和企业始终能获得所需的电力，哪怕是在夏季或冬季的用电高峰期。作为背景参考，在单日内，仅纽约市一地的电力负荷波动就可高达 2 吉瓦（GW），而作为对比基数的日常峰值仅为 6 至 8 吉瓦。当热浪来袭、所有人同时开启空调时，该市的用电负荷甚至会飙升至 10 吉瓦。

understanding.role_in_article: Educates the reader on grid economics, defining the structural difference between PJM's capacity market and ERCOT's energy-only market.
understanding.relation_to_previous: Shifts the focus from the symptoms (high bills, AI blame) to the underlying structural mechanics of how electricity bills are calculated.
understanding.relation_to_next: Provides the necessary technical definitions to understand the magnitude of PJM's $16 billion failure in the subsequent section.
key_points: Capacity markets (like PJM) charge ratepayers to pay power plants to sit idle for extreme peak scenarios.
ERCOT uses an 'energy-only' model relying on real-time scarcity pricing instead of centralized annual capacity auctions.
translation_notes: Terminology is critical: 'Capacity market' (容量市场), 'energy-only market' (单一电量市场/纯电能市场), 'retail adders' (零售附加费), 'ancillary services' (辅助服务).
Ensure the explanation of 'scarcity pricing' (稀缺定价) clearly conveys its role as an incentive mechanism.

paragraphs.source: In PJM, to ensure we have capacity when it’s needed, we pay power plant owners to maintain their assets on standby for >95% of the year. The price paid is determined by a forward auction, run once per year. That price is then spread across all electricity customers in the region. And that price went up by 9.3x in the 2025/26 delivery period, as detailed below.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: In PJM, to ensure we have capacity when it’s needed, we pay power plant owners to maintain their assets on standby for >95% of the year. The price paid is determined by a forward auction, run once per year. That price is then spread across all electricity customers in the region. And that price went up by 9.3x in the 2025/26 delivery period, as detailed below.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: In PJM, to ensure we have capacity when it’s needed, we pay power plant owners to maintain their assets on standby for >95% of the year. The price paid is determined by a forward auction, run once per year. That price is then spread across all electricity customers in the region. And that price went up by 9.3x in the 2025/26 delivery period, as detailed below.

pro.text: 在PJM，为确保在关键时刻有充足的电力容量，我们向发电厂所有者支付费用，让其资产在全年超过95%的时间里处于闲置待命状态。这笔费用的定价由每年举行一次的远期拍卖来决定，随后这笔成本会被分摊给该区域内的所有电力用户。正如后文详述，在2025/26交付期，这一价格暴涨了9.3倍。

paragraphs.source: ERCOT, on the other hand, is an “energy-only” market, with no separate capacity auction. Real-time pricing signals determine “scarcity” and incentivizes power plants to solve the issue. There are a few technicalities we detail later in the report, but the fundamental difference is the absence of a centralized annual capacity auction, instead using real-time market forces.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: ERCOT, on the other hand, is an “energy-only” market, with no separate capacity auction. Real-time pricing signals determine “scarcity” and incentivizes power plants to solve the issue. There are a few technicalities we detail later in the report, but the fundamental difference is the absence of a centralized annual capacity auction, instead using real-time market forces.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: ERCOT, on the other hand, is an “energy-only” market, with no separate capacity auction. Real-time pricing signals determine “scarcity” and incentivizes power plants to solve the issue. There are a few technicalities we detail later in the report, but the fundamental difference is the absence of a centralized annual capacity auction, instead using real-time market forces.

pro.text: 相比之下，ERCOT是一个“纯电能市场”，没有单独的容量拍卖机制。实时价格信号决定了电力的“稀缺性”，并以此激励发电厂解决供需缺口。我们在报告后文中会详述一些技术细节，但两者的根本区别在于，ERCOT不存在中心化的年度容量拍卖，而是依靠实时的市场力量。
